---
title: "nitrix.stats.inference.multiple_comparisons"
generated: true
signature-hash: 44a788242deb6fb4
---

::: {.callout-important title="Generated reference stub ‚Äî fill me"}
Auto-generated scaffold for `nitrix.stats.inference.multiple_comparisons`. Fill the prose *outside* the
generated markers; do **not** ship as-is (SPEC ¬ß6.5). `check_reference_staleness.py` flags signature drift.
:::

<!-- nitrix:gen dims:__page__ -->
**Tensor dimensions**

| dim | meaning |
|---|---|
| `n` | query points, each an `ndim`-vector coordinate (variadic) |
| `k` | kernel or basis components |
| `*spatial` | the spatial axes of a grid (variadic) |
<!-- nitrix:/gen -->

## `fdr` {#fdr}

<!-- nitrix:gen sig:fdr -->
```python
fdr(p_values: Float[Array, 'n'], *, method: FDRMethod = 'bh', alpha: float = 0.05, lam: float = 0.5) -> Tuple[Bool[Array, 'n'], Float[Array, 'n']]
```
<!-- nitrix:/gen -->

<!-- nitrix:gen overview:fdr -->
Unified false-discovery-rate correction.

`method`: `'bh'` (default -- Benjamini-Hochberg, valid under independence
/ PRDS), `'by'` (Benjamini-Yekutieli, valid under arbitrary dependence), or
`'storey'` (pi0-adaptive, higher power; `lam` tunes the `pi0` estimate).
Returns `(rejected, p_adjusted)`.
<!-- nitrix:/gen -->

<!-- nitrix:gen params:fdr -->
**Parameters**

| parameter | type | shape | default | description |
|---|---|---|---|---|
| `p_values` | `Float` | `n` | ‚Äî | ‚Äî |
| `method` | `FDRMethod` | ‚Äî | `'bh'` | ‚Äî |
| `alpha` | `float` | ‚Äî | `0.05` | ‚Äî |
| `lam` | `float` | ‚Äî | `0.5` | ‚Äî |

**Returns** `Tuple[Bool[Array, 'n'], Float[Array, 'n']]`.
<!-- nitrix:/gen -->

## `fdr_bh` {#fdr_bh}

<!-- nitrix:gen sig:fdr_bh -->
```python
fdr_bh(p_values: Float[Array, 'n'], *, alpha: float = 0.05) -> Tuple[Bool[Array, 'n'], Float[Array, 'n']]
```
<!-- nitrix:/gen -->

<!-- nitrix:gen overview:fdr_bh -->
Benjamini-Hochberg FDR correction (valid under independence / PRDS).

Returns `(rejected, p_adjusted)`: the BH-adjusted p-values (q-values,
enforced monotone non-decreasing in rank) and the rejection mask
`p_adjusted <= alpha`.
<!-- nitrix:/gen -->

<!-- nitrix:gen params:fdr_bh -->
**Parameters**

| parameter | type | shape | default | description |
|---|---|---|---|---|
| `p_values` | `Float` | `n` | ‚Äî | ‚Äî |
| `alpha` | `float` | ‚Äî | `0.05` | ‚Äî |

**Returns** `Tuple[Bool[Array, 'n'], Float[Array, 'n']]`.
<!-- nitrix:/gen -->

## `fdr_by` {#fdr_by}

<!-- nitrix:gen sig:fdr_by -->
```python
fdr_by(p_values: Float[Array, 'n'], *, alpha: float = 0.05) -> Tuple[Bool[Array, 'n'], Float[Array, 'n']]
```
<!-- nitrix:/gen -->

<!-- nitrix:gen overview:fdr_by -->
Benjamini-Yekutieli FDR correction -- valid under **arbitrary** dependence.

The BH step-up at level `alpha / c(m)` with the harmonic number
`c(m) = sum_{k=1}^m 1/k` (equivalently, the BH q-values scaled by `c(m)`).
Use when the p-values may be arbitrarily (e.g. negatively) dependent and BH's
positive-dependence assumption is unsafe -- the price is a `~log(m)` factor
more conservative.  Returns `(rejected, p_adjusted)`.
<!-- nitrix:/gen -->

<!-- nitrix:gen params:fdr_by -->
**Parameters**

| parameter | type | shape | default | description |
|---|---|---|---|---|
| `p_values` | `Float` | `n` | ‚Äî | ‚Äî |
| `alpha` | `float` | ‚Äî | `0.05` | ‚Äî |

**Returns** `Tuple[Bool[Array, 'n'], Float[Array, 'n']]`.
<!-- nitrix:/gen -->

## `fdr_storey` {#fdr_storey}

<!-- nitrix:gen sig:fdr_storey -->
```python
fdr_storey(p_values: Float[Array, 'n'], *, alpha: float = 0.05, lam: float = 0.5) -> Tuple[Bool[Array, 'n'], Float[Array, 'n']]
```
<!-- nitrix:/gen -->

<!-- nitrix:gen overview:fdr_storey -->
Storey's pi0-adaptive FDR / q-values (higher power; independence / PRDS).

Benjamini-Hochberg with the count `m` replaced by the estimated number of
true nulls `m * pi0` (see :func:`storey_pi0`): uniformly less conservative
than BH when a non-trivial fraction of effects are real, and identical to BH
when `pi0 = 1`.  Returns `(rejected, p_adjusted)`.
<!-- nitrix:/gen -->

<!-- nitrix:gen params:fdr_storey -->
**Parameters**

| parameter | type | shape | default | description |
|---|---|---|---|---|
| `p_values` | `Float` | `n` | ‚Äî | ‚Äî |
| `alpha` | `float` | ‚Äî | `0.05` | ‚Äî |
| `lam` | `float` | ‚Äî | `0.5` | ‚Äî |

**Returns** `Tuple[Bool[Array, 'n'], Float[Array, 'n']]`.
<!-- nitrix:/gen -->

## `storey_pi0` {#storey_pi0}

<!-- nitrix:gen sig:storey_pi0 -->
```python
storey_pi0(p_values: Float[Array, 'n'], *, lam: float = 0.5) -> Float[Array, '']
```
<!-- nitrix:/gen -->

<!-- nitrix:gen overview:storey_pi0 -->
Storey's estimate of the true-null proportion `pi0` (Storey 2002).

`pi0(lambda) = #{p_i > lambda} / (m (1 - lambda))`, clipped to
`[1/m, 1]`.  The tuning `lambda in (0, 1)` (default `0.5`) trades bias
for variance; `pi0 = 1` recovers Benjamini-Hochberg, and a smaller `pi0`
(more alternatives present) yields a less conservative, higher-power adaptive
FDR.  The `1/m` floor guards the degenerate "no null" estimate.
<!-- nitrix:/gen -->

<!-- nitrix:gen params:storey_pi0 -->
**Parameters**

| parameter | type | shape | default | description |
|---|---|---|---|---|
| `p_values` | `Float` | `n` | ‚Äî | ‚Äî |
| `lam` | `float` | ‚Äî | `0.5` | ‚Äî |

**Returns** `Float` ‚Äî shape ‚Äî.
<!-- nitrix:/gen -->

## `bonferroni` {#bonferroni}

<!-- nitrix:gen sig:bonferroni -->
```python
bonferroni(p_values: Float[Array, 'n'], *, alpha: float = 0.05) -> Tuple[Bool[Array, 'n'], Float[Array, 'n']]
```
<!-- nitrix:/gen -->

<!-- nitrix:gen overview:bonferroni -->
Bonferroni family-wise correction.

Returns `(rejected, p_adjusted)` with `p_adjusted = min(p * n, 1)`.
<!-- nitrix:/gen -->

<!-- nitrix:gen params:bonferroni -->
**Parameters**

| parameter | type | shape | default | description |
|---|---|---|---|---|
| `p_values` | `Float` | `n` | ‚Äî | ‚Äî |
| `alpha` | `float` | ‚Äî | `0.05` | ‚Äî |

**Returns** `Tuple[Bool[Array, 'n'], Float[Array, 'n']]`.
<!-- nitrix:/gen -->

## `conjunction` {#conjunction}

<!-- nitrix:gen sig:conjunction -->
```python
conjunction(stats: Float[Array, 'k *spatial']) -> Float[Array, '*spatial']
```
<!-- nitrix:/gen -->

<!-- nitrix:gen overview:conjunction -->
Minimum-statistic conjunction across `k` contrasts (Nichols et al. 2005).

`stats` stacks the `k` per-contrast statistic maps on the leading axis.
Returns the per-element **minimum** over contrasts -- the valid statistic for
the conjunction null *"at least one of the `k` effects is null"*: a voxel
survives a threshold only where **every** contrast clears it (the minimum
does).  This is the correct conjunction; the common
"all-effects-null" intersection of separately-thresholded maps tests a
*different* (global) null and does not control the conjunction error.
<!-- nitrix:/gen -->

<!-- nitrix:gen params:conjunction -->
**Parameters**

| parameter | type | shape | default | description |
|---|---|---|---|---|
| `stats` | `Float` | `k` √ó `*spatial` | ‚Äî | ‚Äî |

**Returns** `Float` ‚Äî shape `*spatial`.
<!-- nitrix:/gen -->

## `conjunction_pvalue` {#conjunction_pvalue}

<!-- nitrix:gen sig:conjunction_pvalue -->
```python
conjunction_pvalue(p_values: Float[Array, 'k *spatial']) -> Float[Array, '*spatial']
```
<!-- nitrix:/gen -->

<!-- nitrix:gen overview:conjunction_pvalue -->
Conjunction p-value: the **maximum** per-contrast p-value over `k`
contrasts (the p-scale dual of :func:`conjunction`).

The minimum-statistic conjunction is significant at `alpha` iff every
contrast's p-value is below `alpha` iff their maximum is -- so `max p` is
the valid conjunction p-value (Nichols et al. 2005).
<!-- nitrix:/gen -->

<!-- nitrix:gen params:conjunction_pvalue -->
**Parameters**

| parameter | type | shape | default | description |
|---|---|---|---|---|
| `p_values` | `Float` | `k` √ó `*spatial` | ‚Äî | ‚Äî |

**Returns** `Float` ‚Äî shape `*spatial`.
<!-- nitrix:/gen -->
